calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-20-2026 Continued To Jul-29-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion To Voluntarily Dismiss Without Prejudice
Off Calendar Jun-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' unopposed Motion To Voluntarily Dismiss Without Prejudice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Plaintiffs move under California Rule of Court, rule 3.770. "A dismissal of an entire class action, or of any party or cause of action in a class action, requires court approval." (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.770(a).)

The court notes the pending action, Nelson v. Tea Dating Advice, Inc., San Diego County Super. Court Case No. 25CU045010 and the parties' commitment to consolidate this action with Nelson. Good cause appearing, the court grants Plaintiffs leave to dismiss this action. The class has not been certified. And the court finds dismissal will not prejudice class members. The court, therefore, will not require notice to the class at this time. (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.770(c).) Plaintiffs must file a CIV-110.

In advance of the hearing time, Plaintiff is ordered to submit by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the above verbatim.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Continued To Jun-17-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
